title: Online identity theft - distinguishing it from regular fraud
p1: Most people think fraud and identity theft are the same thing. They are not—and the difference changes how you protect yourself online.
p2: A common misconception is that both only involve stolen money. But identity theft can happen long before any transaction occurs. Your data can be quietly collected, combined, and even sold, without you seeing a single charge. Many people say, 'I didn't lose anything, so I must be safe'—that’s the risky part.
p3: Identity theft is about impersonation—someone using your personal details to act as you. Fraud is usually a single unauthorized transaction. The key difference is scope: one attack takes money, the other can take your entire digital identity. A simple step is enabling alerts on your accounts and reviewing logins regularly so unusual activity doesn’t go unnoticed.
conclusion: Have you ever noticed a login or account change you didn’t make—and wondered whether it was fraud or identity misuse?
